mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future earnings, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS declines, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 7.0% signals severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate profit growth, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >70% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, or signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) over 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 1.0% quarter-over-quarter reflects strong manufacturing output that boosts earnings expectations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Signal predicts negative returns during late-cycle expansions, or 20-day forward returns show no significant difference (p > 0.05) between high and low INDPRO growth regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPIAUCSL inflation readings exceeding 2.5% trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in >65% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 228.0, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening that raises wage costs and compresses profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or interaction effect Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth rates above 2.0% reflect sustained economic expansion that supports corporate earnings trajectories, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index falling below 95.0 indicates a manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate revenue forecasts, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >70% of instances where INDPRO drops below 95.0, or signal Rank IC < 0.01.
